channel.title: McKinsey Insights & Publications
channel.copyright: Copyright (c) McKinsey & Company. All rights reserved. Reprinted by permission.
channel.generator: Sitecore CMS: http://www.sitecore.net. Sitecore RSS module: McKinsey.RSS, Version=1.3.2.9663, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=null

item.title: Travel Disruptors: Bringing the low-cost airline model to Türkiye
item.description: Pegasus Airlines has achieved considerable growth as a low-cost carrier. CEO Güliz Öztürk discusses its key strategic choices.

item.title: The Josh Harris playbook: building cultures of excellence in business and sports
item.description: The founder of 26North shares his thoughts on leadership, starting anew at 55, and the power of sports to create community in today’s world.

item.title: Lykke Hinsch Gylvin on the cutting edge of clinical trial design
item.description: Lykke Hinsch Gylvin shares how Boehringer Ingelheim’s innovative clinical trial designs bring therapies to patients faster.

item.title: Exploring the frontier: Technology trends in Asia
item.description: Cutting-edge technologies present exciting possibilities for organizations globally and in Asia, but their full potential needs to be unlocked.

item.title: You move at the speed of trust: Admiral Thad Allen
item.description: Admiral Thad Allen reflects on what it takes to be a public service leader—with trust, transparency, emotional intelligence, and self-learning emerging as the most important traits.

item.title: Demystifying top-team performance: What every CEO needs to know
item.description: Building a healthy, high-performing top team doesn’t happen by accident—it’s a deliberate, ongoing process. CEOs who commit to this priority can build a sustainable competitive advantage over time.

item.title: The holistic impact of family organizations
item.description: Family-owned businesses, including their overall family organizations, have the potential to create a multifaceted impact that extends beyond their economic contribution and job creation.

item.title: Don’t miss the bus: New strategies for European bus and coach OEMs
item.description: European bus and coach manufacturers are revving up for a revolution as transportation becomes cleaner, greener, and more competitive.

item.title: Transforming an underperforming business before separation
item.description: How do top performers get the most for their lackluster assets? A systematic review of an asset’s full business potential, bottom-up planning, and transformation implementation are critical.

item.title: How CPGs can rethink costs to regain performance
item.description: In a low-volume-growth world, a new approach to cost cutting called “fit to win” can reduce expenses, expand margins, and help CPGs find new top-line momentum.

item.title: The change agent: Goals, decisions, and implications for CEOs in the agentic age
item.description: Companies are feeling agentic AI growing pains. Here’s what CEOs can do to move past them and position their companies to succeed.

item.title: The future of the CLO: Leading in a world of merged work and learning
item.description: Technology has given chief learning officers the tools to make learning a seamless and integrated part of the work experience. To succeed, CLOs can make several fundamental changes.
item.category: Insights on People and Organizational Performance

item.title: Talking with Daragh Murphy, founder and CEO of Imprint
item.description: What it takes to build a cobranded credit card business on a new technology stack during a time of intense uncertainty.

item.title: Winning in sustainable packaging in 2025: Bringing it all together
item.description: How do incumbents win in sustainable packaging today? Based on a decade of research, we provide a strategic framework on where to play—and how—to overcome barriers and seize opportunities.

item.title: Sanjiva Weerawarana on building products in international markets
item.description: Sanjiva Weerawarana discusses the principles he’s followed while leading WSO2 from its humble beginnings in Sri Lanka to its current presence in 11 countries.

item.title: Grant management: Boosting efficiency, accountability, and impact
item.description: By addressing challenges across the grant management life cycle, organizations such as government agencies, public institutions, and philanthropies can maximize the impact of available grant funding.

item.title: China Auto Consumer Insights 2025: Gaining momentum
item.description: Four major trends are reshaping the Chinese auto industry. As innovation and new technologies take center stage, OEMs need to combine globalized platforms with localized scenarios.

item.title: The McKinsey Crossword: Winging It | No. 252
item.description: Sharpen your problem-solving skills the McKinsey way, with our weekly crossword. Each puzzle is created with the McKinsey audience in mind, and includes a subtle (and sometimes not-so-subtle) business theme for you to find. Answers that are directionally correct may not cut it if you’re looking for a quick win.

item.title: The transformative potential of AI in Romania’s public sector
item.description: To capitalize on gen AI, Romania needs to build on its fast-growing preparedness for AI adoption, potentially delivering annual public sector productivity gains of up to €1 billion.

item.title: Climate resilience technology: An inflection point for new investment
item.description: Demand for technologies that support climate resilience and adaptation could create a $1 trillion opportunity for private capital by 2030.

item.title: Survey results: Expectations for company performance, by industry
item.description: Surveyed executives in many industries predict stable company performance in the months ahead. Those working in professional services are more skeptical.

item.title: Economic conditions outlook, September 2025
item.description: Surveyed executives continue to see shifts in trade policy as a disruptive force globally and locally, and their companies are exploring areas of opportunity in response.

item.title: The 2025 McKinsey Global Payments Report: Competing systems, contested outcomes
item.description: This year’s report dissects the rise of diverse payment rails, the impact of digital assets, and the transformative power of AI, offering a road map for success in a rapidly evolving global ecosystem.

item.title: Leading a best-in-class health system integration
item.description: The CEO and the president of BJC Health System discuss the challenges of combining two large organizations and the critical role of culture and leadership in achieving success.

item.title: The agentic organization: Contours of the next paradigm for the AI era
item.description: Companies are moving toward a new paradigm of humans working together with virtual and physical AI agents to create value. We share lessons from early adopters—and what you can do next.
item.category: Insights on People and Organizational Performance

item.title: Beyond the wheel: Perspectives on autonomous vehicles
item.description: The rise of self-driving cars is about more than technology—it’s about reshaping trust, infrastructure, and everyday life.

item.title: The promise and potential of rural America
item.description: Rural communities account for more than 70 percent of US land and generate over $2 trillion in economic output. How can we ensure that scale is matched by opportunity for the people who live there?

item.title: Global Economics Intelligence executive summary, August 2025
item.description: Uncertainty moderated in August, with several tariff actions finalized. However, persistent economic strains heighten concerns in Europe, and revised US data signals a softer labor market.

item.title: Attracting Gen Z talent into US public service
item.description: Two new surveys suggest 12 solutions for public service organizations looking to attract and retain the next generation of workers.

item.title: Finance solutions for nature: Pathways to returns and outcomes
item.description: Ten finance solutions can mainstream nature in capital markets.

item.title: From surplus to scarcity: K–12 districts brace for leaner years
item.description: Declining enrollment and funding, rising costs, and increasing student needs are putting public school districts under pressure. Our latest survey reveals K–12 leaders’ concerns and reactions.

item.title: Talent wins: The growing business of women’s sports
item.description: With new leagues, growing audiences, and rising valuations, women’s sports is scaling fast. Infrastructure, strategy, and investment are helping transform it into a sustainable, high-growth industry.
item.category: Insights on People and Organizational Performance

item.title: DBS CEO Tan Su Shan on building a gen AI–enabled bank with a heart
item.description: The new CEO says the bank is going all in on AI, but it is also committed to being an empathetic organization, in line with its development bank roots.

item.title: The McKinsey Crossword: Lives Well Led | No. 251
item.description: Sharpen your problem-solving skills the McKinsey way, with our weekly crossword. Each puzzle is created with the McKinsey audience in mind, and includes a subtle (and sometimes not-so-subtle) business theme for you to find. Answers that are directionally correct may not cut it if you’re looking for a quick win.

item.title: The new case for brain health: Scaling interventions for health and economic growth
item.description: Opportunities exist to reduce the global burden of brain health and noncommunicable diseases, fostering healthier communities and stronger economies.

item.title: Upgrading software business models to thrive in the AI era
item.description: AI is transforming software from a tool that enables work into a platform that actively performs and orchestrates work. How should industry players evolve their business models to lead in this new era?

item.title: The FDI shake-up: How foreign direct investment today may shape industry and trade tomorrow
item.description: Announced FDI projects signal further changes in the geometry of global trade and the future map of international business.

item.title: Ocean carbon dioxide removal: What’s on the horizon?
item.description: Ocean CO2 removal offers opportunities for a range of stakeholders. While significant development is needed, these unique climate technologies could be game-changing at scale—here’s how.

item.title: Assembling a sustainable built environment
item.description: Stakeholders from real estate owners to material manufacturers stand to gain from a more carbon-friendly construction industry. Here’s how the sector can reach its net-zero goals.

item.title: McKinsey’s India office founder, a philanthropic billionaire cheese maker, and a champion of Afghan girls
item.description: Our latest obituaries also include a Major League Baseball draftee turned corporate CEO, an advertising Hall of Famer, and a cognitive scientist who made cutting-edge AI predictions decades ago.

item.title: Riding the hyperscaler wave: The investment opportunity in cloud ecosystems
item.description: The underlying market for cloud infrastructure could reach $3.4 trillion by 2040. Capturing the compelling associated investment opportunity in cloud partner ecosystems requires a winning strategy.

item.title: The UK insurtech landscape: Strong, shifting, collaborative
item.description: The UK insurtech landscape is continuously evolving. Our latest survey discovers how funding has shifted toward value chain enablers and the opportunities new technologies are presenting.

item.title: Keeping investment in mind: Strategies for financing mental health
item.description: Effectively funding mental health requires both additional investments to close the funding gap and sustainable financing approaches for long-term success.

item.title: Asset management 2025: The great convergence
item.description: As the industry rebounds, the lines between traditional and alternative asset management are blurring.

item.title: To unleash productivity growth in Europe, rewire your operations
item.description: Slowing productivity growth in Europe keeps leaders awake at night. A better dawn can come—if businesses commit to major change.

item.title: Global Lighthouse voices: Chow Woai Sheng on Agilent’s 4IR evolution
item.description: Chow Woai Sheng explains how Agilent aims to advance exceptional science with next-generation technology to boost sustainable, agile, and resilient manufacturing.

item.title: What is infrastructure?
item.description: Infrastructure is no longer just bridges, roads, and the like—it now includes elements ranging from digital networks to clean-energy systems to electric-vehicle charging corridors.

item.title: From crisis to catalyst: Investing in addressing noncommunicable diseases
item.description: Without urgent action, noncommunicable diseases could cost the world $36 trillion—but scaling interventions that work can both reverse the trend and serve as an engine for economic growth.

item.title: The McKinsey Crossword: H Rhymes | No. 250
item.description: Sharpen your problem-solving skills the McKinsey way, with our weekly crossword. Each puzzle is created with the McKinsey audience in mind, and includes a subtle (and sometimes not-so-subtle) business theme for you to find. Answers that are directionally correct may not cut it if you’re looking for a quick win.

item.title: Remapping travel with agentic AI
item.description: Agentic AI could upend the travel industry. Travel and hospitality organizations should explore how agentic can help catalyze AI’s transformative potential.
